title: Sinclair/Life Biosciences confirmed by University of Nicosia: first human trial of cellular-aging gene therapy now active
description: Following last week's FDA clearance, David Sinclair was conferred Doctor Honoris Causa at University of Nicosia on June 5, with the university confirming that Life Biosciences' partial-reprogramming gene therapy has reached first-in-human trials in glaucoma and NAION. This shifts the Information Theory of Aging from hypothesis to live clinical program.

title: Sinclair and Tally Health publish first comprehensive evidence map of human epigenetic aging interventions in Frontiers in Genetics
description: The largest published synthesis to date (41 human studies) identifies exercise, semaglutide, pitavastatin, plant-rich diet, caloric restriction, omega-3, and multivitamin as interventions associated with favorable next-generation epigenetic clock changes. The review provides the field's first rigorous separation of evidence-backed from unproven longevity protocols.

title: Semaglutide slows biological aging in randomized controlled trial — Nature Communications
description: The McComsey/Corley team published the first RCT showing semaglutide slows epigenetic aging across multiple clocks in adults with HIV lipohypertrophy, with effects spanning inflammation and the heart, brain, kidneys, liver, blood, and metabolic system. This is the strongest human evidence yet that a GLP-1 RA directly affects molecular aging.

title: Forever Healthy Foundation launches Evipedia.ai — open evidence encyclopedia for 500+ longevity interventions
description: Michael Greve's Forever Healthy Foundation released Evipedia.ai, an open online encyclopedia covering in-depth evidence reviews of more than 500 health and longevity interventions including first-generation rejuvenation therapies, supplements, botanicals, and lifestyle protocols. The launch fills a long-standing gap in unbiased, structured longevity evidence aggregation.

title: Cross-species transcriptomic aging clock validated in Nature
description: A new Nature paper integrated more than 11,000 gene-activity samples across four mammal species to define a universal aging signature, identifying CDKN1A and LGALS3 as markers that track death risk and disease in UK Biobank data. Caloric restriction slowed mitochondrial aging while chronic disease accelerated inflammatory aging — a key step toward standardized cross-species geroscience endpoints.

title: Sinclair and Tally Health: comprehensive evidence map of human epigenetic aging interventions
description: Frontiers in Genetics review analyzing 41 human studies identifies exercise, semaglutide, pitavastatin, plant-rich diet, caloric restriction, omega-3, multivitamin and emerging clinical therapies as interventions associated with favorable changes in next-generation epigenetic aging clocks.

title: Semaglutide slows epigenetic aging in randomized HIV lipohypertrophy trial
description: First randomized placebo-controlled clinical evidence that semaglutide slows the accumulation of DNA-level biological aging markers, published in Nature Communications.

title: Popular weight-loss drug found to slow biological aging in landmark human trial
description: Coverage of the Corley et al. RCT in Nature Communications showing semaglutide slows epigenetic aging across heart, brain, kidney, liver, blood, and metabolic clocks compared to placebo.

title: NewLimit raises $435M Series C at $3.1B valuation to fund first human cell-reprogramming trial
description: Founders Fund-led round (Thrive Capital, Lilly Ventures, Nat Friedman, Daniel Gross) brings the Brian Armstrong-founded company to a $3.1B valuation ahead of its first clinical trial of a liver age-reversal medicine.

title: NewLimit raises $435M to rewrite longevity science
description: Detailed funding-history coverage: NewLimit's $435M Series C follows a $130M Series B in 2025 and $45M extension, positioning the company to begin first human clinical trial of cell age reprogramming next year.

title: NewLimit Raises $435M as Longevity Medicine Accelerates
description: Industry framing of NewLimit's raise alongside Retro Biosciences ($1.8B valuation, Sam Altman) and Bezos-backed Altos Labs, underscoring AI-enabled longevity medicine as a top venture theme.

title: Harvard's first longevity report brings geroscience to a mass audience
description: Harvard Health Publishing's first longevity special report frames geroscience, biological aging and preventive longevity medicine for general consumers, signaling field-wide legitimization.

title: University of Nicosia confers Honorary Doctorate on David Sinclair; confirms first human gene-therapy trial active
description: Sinclair's UNIC Athens Evolve Lecture announces that Life Biosciences' partial-reprogramming therapy has reached first human trials in glaucoma and NAION, with the university conferring Doctor of Philosophy Honoris Causa.

title: Sinclair: FDA clearance for first human gene-therapy trial to reverse cellular aging
description: Dr. Sinclair confirms FDA clearance for the first human trial of a gene therapy designed to reverse cellular aging, tied to the Information Theory of Aging framework.

title: Cross-species transcriptomic aging clock published in Nature
description: Universal aging signatures across mice, monkeys and humans (>11,000 gene activity samples) identify CDKN1A and LGALS3 as markers tracking mortality and multiple diseases in UK Biobank data.

title: Fight Aging!: Deriving insight into aging from gene networks
description: Coverage of new work using gene network analyses to derive mechanistic insight into aging — methodological foundation for future intervention discovery.

title: Forever Healthy Foundation launches Evipedia.ai
description: Open online encyclopedia covering 500+ health and longevity interventions including first-generation rejuvenation therapies, supplements, botanicals, and lifestyle protocols — built by the Michael Greve Forever Healthy Foundation.

title: The Thalion Initiative: a new non-profit with big ambitions
description: Lifespan.io profiles The Thalion Initiative, a newly launched non-profit aimed at accelerating longevity research and advocacy.

title: Longevity Technology Roundup Week 20: GLP-1s in progressive MS, executive longevity care, AI skin regeneration
description: Neuraly's NLY01 begins Phase 2 in progressive MS using brain atrophy MRI as primary endpoint; Biograph executive assessments scale; Scarlet Therapeutics raises $4M for lab-grown red blood cells; Horvath sees convergence in aging biomarkers.

title: Peter Attia: The beginning of the end of atherosclerosis?
description: Attia outlines current tools (exercise, sleep hygiene, diet, statin-era medications) and emerging technologies that could render atherosclerosis a tractable rather than dominant cause of death.

title: Peter Attia Drive #394 — Sleep pharmacology and the role of medications in healthy aging
description: Episode 394 of The Drive details when and how sleep medications fit into longevity-oriented sleep optimization, with an emphasis on long-term safety.

title: Kaeberlein: AGE2026 Day 1 takeaways — mTOR/rapamycin dominate, field pivoting to translation
description: Kaeberlein reports that mTOR/rapamycin work spanning immune function, ovarian aging, brain health, and metabolism continues to lead AGE conference programming, with rigorous human translation now the field's central focus.

title: Rapamycin in endometriosis and fertility: a new frontier
description: Detailed clinical synthesis of Columbia VIBRANT and 2025 Li et al. Cell Reports Medicine RCT showing rapamycin (1 mg/day for 21–28 days) raised IVF clinical pregnancy rate from 28% to 50% in poor responders, alongside guidance on dosing and CYP3A4 caveats.

title: Dasatinib + quercetin causes brain myelin damage in mice — UConn safety warning
description: UConn researchers show D+Q senolytic at standard longevity doses triggers significant demyelination in corpus callosum white matter in young and old mice, raising urgent concerns about off-label use in humans.

title: Two national canine clinical trials of anti-aging drugs underway: rapamycin and LOY-002
description: Two national US trials are evaluating rapamycin and the less-disclosed LOY-002 in pet dogs as a translational model for healthspan extension in humans.

title: Strength training: 90–119 minutes/week hits longevity sweet spot — 13% lower all-cause mortality
description: In nearly 150,000 adults tracked up to 30 years, 90–119 minutes/week of strength training produced the optimal mortality benefit; more added no extra advantage. Pairing with aerobic exercise gave the largest reduction.

title: Staying active from midlife cuts sarcopenia risk by up to 78%
description: 30-year Norwegian cohort study shows those who remained active from middle age into their 70s had 78% lower odds of confirmed muscle loss; even late starters saw meaningful benefit.

title: Low vitamin D linked to sarcopenia, especially in people with diabetes
description: Cross-sectional analysis of 7,500+ older adults links low vitamin D to higher odds of sarcopenia; rat experiments showed vitamin D3 supplementation improved muscle strength and reduced intramuscular fat.

title: 15-PGDH inhibitor preserves muscle during semaglutide weight loss in obese mice
description: Adding a 15-PGDH inhibitor to GLP-1 therapy preserved muscle repair and regrowth while retaining weight-loss benefits in obese mice — a potential adjunct to maintain lean mass on Ozempic-class drugs.

title: Nature: How long can humans live? — Saul Newman's Morbid challenges supercentenarian claims
description: Newman argues celebrated supercentenarian cases rest on faulty records and calls for physics-based age verification (amino-acid racemization, radiocarbon) to calibrate biomarker age estimates against ground truth.
